title: Fetch on Mount
description:
This component should load a list of users once, when it mounts. Instead it calls `fetch` directly in the render body — so it fires on every render, never awaits the response, and never stores the result.

Move the data loading into a `useEffect` that runs once (empty dependency array), `await` the response, and store it with `setUsers`.
objectives: await the fetch and the `.json()`, then call `setUsers(...)`
hints: Side effects (like fetching) belong in `useEffect`, not in the render body.
An empty dependency array `[]` makes the effect run once on mount.
fetch is async: `const res = await fetch(url); const data = await res.json();`. Define an async function inside the effect and call it.
